key_insight: For small language models, step-level process rewards outperform final-answer rewards in RLVR for math reasoning, improving both accuracy and trace quality.
engineering_relevance: When designing reward functions for small-model RL training, reward granularity is a critical knob that can materially change convergence, robustness, and error modes.
method: Fine-tuned Qwen2.5-0.5B with Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) on GSM8K.
Compared no-RL, process-only, outcome-only, and three hybrid process-weight settings (λ = 0.9, 0.5, 0.1).
Used GPT-4o-based error analysis to compare trace validity and failure-mode patterns.
evidence_signal: On GSM8K, process-only supervision reached 63.73% test accuracy versus 53.75% for outcome-only, with hybrids generally improving as process weight increased.
watch_item: The results are shown on a single small model and benchmark, so the process-vs-outcome advantage may not transfer unchanged to larger models or other math tasks.
paper_title: Reward Granularity in RLVR: Comparing Process and Outcome Reward Structures for Mathematical Reasoning in Small Language Models
